incorrects.description: Student set wrong number of containers on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. How many groups do we need? Listen again: 3 groups of 4.

incorrects.description: Student set wrong number of containers on second attempt

params.description: Number 3 highlights on container count selector.

steps.text: Let's think about this together. 3 groups means 3 bags. Look at the number 3 on the selector.

incorrects.description: Student set wrong number of containers three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System sets container count to 3. 3 bags appear in workspace.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. We need 3 groups. That means 3 bags. I set the number to 3, and 3 bags appear. The answer is 3 groups.

incorrects.description: Student set wrong number of items per container on first attempt

steps.text: Almost. How many in each group? We need 3 groups of 4.

incorrects.description: Student set wrong number of items per container on second attempt

params.description: Number 4 highlights on items per container selector.

steps.text: Here's a hint: 3 groups of 4 means each bag gets 4 items. Find the number 4 on the selector.

incorrects.description: Student set wrong number of items per container three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System sets items per container to 4. 4 items appear in each of the 3 bags.

steps.text: Here, let me walk you through this one. We need 4 in each group. I set the number to 4. Now each bag has 4 items inside. The answer is 4 items in each group.
